stem: A drug candidate enters the drug development pipeline. The probability of passing preclinical testing is 90%. If the previous stage is passed, the probability of passing Phase I trial is between 59% and 72% (uncertain due to evaluator variability). If the previous stage is passed, the probability of passing Phase II trial is 92%. What is the overall probability that the drug candidate is approved for market?

ground_truth.answer: P(approved for market) ranges from 0.489 to 0.596 as the Phase I trial pass rate varies from 59% to 72%. Point estimate: 0.546.

solution_steps: The drug candidate must pass all 3 stages sequentially: P(success) = P(stage 1)×P(stage 2)×P(stage 3).
The uncertainty in the Phase I trial stage creates a 10.7 percentage point range in the overall success probability.
common_failure_modes: Adding probabilities instead of multiplying for sequential stages
Using a single value for the Phase I trial rate without acknowledging uncertainty
Not recognizing that uncertainty at one stage propagates through all subsequent stages
